instructions.text: Bring water to a boil
Boil eggs for 12 min
Put eggs in cold water for 2 min, and then peel
Squeeze and mash the peeled eggs in a bowl with your hand (can use a glove for this)
Add the rest of the ingredients in and mix thoroughly

recipes.name: 오이무짐
recipes.description: A sweet and spicy cucumber side dish.
recipes.author: me

instructions.text: Cut the cucumbers into semicircles, roughly 1/4 inches thick
Salt and toss the cucumbers (salting before adding the other stuff enhances texture and flavor)
Mix everything else into a sauce
Add sauce to cucumbers and toss/mix

recipes.name: Chipotle chicken
recipes.description: A chipotle chicken recipe from a former employee. Tastes pretty much the same.
recipes.author: me

ingredients.item: blended chipotle peppers in adobo (brand: San Marcos)

instructions.text: Mix the seasoning and adobo
Marinate the chicken overnight (12+ hours for enhanced flavor)
Coat the chicken in olive oil (throw on a glove and just massage the oil into the bowl of chicken)
Lay the chicken on a rack and bake at 425F for ~40 minutes
Chop chicken thighs up into pieces
Make some quesadillas, burritos, tacos, etc.

recipes.name: 만두
recipes.description: A simple, classic Korean dumpling recipe
recipes.author: me

instructions.text: Soak glass noodles in cold water (1 hour)
Mince napa cabbage and squeeze all of the water out (every last drop)
Mince glass noodles
Finely chop chives and green onions
Mix garlic, ginger, soy sauce, sesame oil, sugar, kosher salt, black pepper
Put everything together and knead with hands/gloves until the meat protein develops a sticky, tacky paste
Wrap dumplings up, place neatly on a backing sheet lined with parchment paper
Freeze the baking sheet, then put the frozen dumplings into a freezer bag

recipes.name: 짜장면
recipes.description: Korean black bean noodles. Eat with 단무지 and lightly salted sunny side up eggs
recipes.author: me

instructions.text: Marinate the meat in some soy sauce (overnight)
Chop the onions into squares (about the size of your thumbnail)
Mix the jjajang powder and water to make a sludge/slurry (can add more powder later depending on how thick you want the sauce)
Stir fry the meat and minced garlic in a pot until the liquid is reduced (if the meat is tough, pour some water and let it braise for a bit before stir frying again)
While there is still a little liquid left, throw the onions in and continue stir frying
Pour water into the pot, just enough to barely cover everything
Turn off the heat, mix the jjajang slurry and sesame oil in (should have the consistency of a thick porridge, it should pretty much glob off your spoon)
Cook some noodles and enjoy

recipes.name: 고추장 돼지불고기
recipes.description: Spicy pork bulgogi. Wrap it up in lettuce with some rice and 쌈장
recipes.author: me

instructions.text: Prep all the ingredients (except the onions) and mix to make marinade
Marinate pork overnight (12+ hours)
Get a pot or pan with a lid (needed to braise the pork shoulder a bit to tenderize it further)
Slice the onions
Cook the pork until it's almost done, then add a little water and cover with a lid (braise for ~10 min)
Remove the lid, add the sliced onions, and finish cooking
Wrap some rice, pork, and 쌈장 in a lettuce leaf. Eat the whole thing in 1 bite.

recipes.name: 닭죽
recipes.description: Korean chicken and rice porridge. Has nice chunks of protein. Be prepared to eat a lot of this.
recipes.author: me

ingredients.item: cubed chicken breast (cubed roughly 1/2 inch thick)

instructions.text: Cook the rice. Cube the chicken and marinate with salt and soy sauce (1-2 hours)
In a large stock pot add the marinated chicken, ginger, garlic.
Add water until it covers the top by 1 inch.
Just as the water starts boiling, bring it down to a simmer.
Put a lid on the pot and simmer for 30 minutes.
Put the cooked rice into a mixing bowl, add some of the stock from the pot, and break up the clumps of rice. (cooks more evenly)
Put in the carrots, peas, and cooked rice into the stock pot.
Add water until it covers the top by 1 inch.
Bring the water to a boil, and then down to a simmer again.
Put the lid back on and simmer for ~1 hour (stir every 10 min to prevent the bottom from burning)
Add more salt or soy sauce if needed. Throw a slice of American cheese in too.

recipes.name: 가른 불고기
recipes.description: Ground beef bulgogi, eat over rice or as a side dish. Easy for meal prep.
recipes.author: me

instructions.text: Mix everything together (except the onions) and marinate overnight.
Slice the onions (and carrots) thinly right before cooking.
Cook the marinated ground beef.
When its almost done cooking, thow in the onions (and carrots) and finish cooking.
Garnish with thinly sliced green onions and some sesame seeds.
Mix everything together (except the broccoli) and marinate overnight.
Wash and separate broccoli florets right before cooking. (slice the stem and pull apart)
Blanch the broccoli for 1 min. Rinse and keep in cold water.
Cook the beef.
When it's almost done, put in a little water, cover it with a lid, and braise for 10 min or until it becomes tender.
When it's almost done, put in a little water, cover it with a lid, and braise for 10 min or until it becomes tender.
Take off the lid and let the liquid reduce a little before adding in the broccoli.
Turn up the heat and finish cooking. Sprinkle in some sesame seeds if you want.
Cook the rice in the rice cooker (use a little less water so the grains dont stick together).
Pour 1 gallon of water into a large bowl.
Put the malt into a strainer/cheesecloth bag and knead it around in the bowl of water for a couple min.
Leave the bag in the bowl and let the sediment settle.
Pour a little bit of the malt water into the rice cooker and break up the rice.
Put the bag of malt in the rice cooker, fill with malt water to the top line/max capacity.

Close the rice cooker and keep warm for 4 - 6 hours (keeps temp between 122F-150F, the point is to keep temp so the amalyse enzyme in the barley malt breaks down the starches from the rice into natural sugars).
Check to see if > 20 grains of rice are floating on the surface.
Put everything into a large stock pot (the bag, the rice, the malt water, the sediment from before, the rest of the ingredients).
Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer for 10 min.
Take out the bag and other ingredients.
Let the pot cool down before filtering out the amount of rice/sediment wanted.
Store in fridge up to 1 week.
